text: Achieving economic growth in line with sustainable development has become a priority for virtually all countries, developed and developing. The economic growth that accompanied industrialisation over the past two centuries was achieved at the cost of huge environmental degradation due to the unplanned character of resource use and the dependence on fossil fuels. The benefits of this growth have not been evenly spread and the resulting employment has not kept pace with the growing labour force. The study analyses the development of so-called green jobs since the late 1980s as a means of addressing both the environmental challenge and the labour force challenge. The study examines the changes in the labour market and how an economy can be restructured to achieve environmentally-friendly growth and decent work and improve the living and working conditions of people and thus ensure sustainable employment and greater economic stability.
